@theshre @soleio @meetgranola @Amplitude_HQ Started with Granola on Windows / iPhone couple days ago 

Love the UX in general, but still can’t get the logic behind its transcription system on Windows.. 

Had a 2-hour chat yesterday -> did a mic test (worked) -> launched the transcript -> after it ended, I can’t extract the transcript nor the audio file (which I could transcribe through any other agent) 

So, it seems that the conversation was lost forever me. But as long as I have this screenshot - I believe it’s not. 

So many questions 😅

tweets.text:
@meetgranola We wanna do a banger OOH campaign and social campaign for you guys. 

Before you see this and leave me on read, we have a philosophy that great OOH campaigns underutilize social to maximize vitality and impressions. 

For example, you guys had this awesome bus stop ad, but I can’t find it anywhere on your socials. 

No campaign to promote it on your own socials and make a funny moment out of it to acquire more customers and impressions. 

We want to help with that. Let us know if you’re open to the convo for the next OOH campaign you do.

It keeps track of all conversations I have had with people including career conversations, team metrics, decisions, patterns (good and bad) and incidents.

Hard to show the structure because there is a lot of personal info there, but it organically fills out the below folders from the above sources.

profile_bio.description: forward deployed idea guy @lasagna_studio | prev marketing @frogdesign

tweets.text:
Last Friday, @smallest_AI hosted a private dinner for community and GTM leaders building in AI-native communications - with friends from @stripe, @meetgranola, @AnthropicAI, @PLAUDAI, @rootlyhq, and @SentientAGI.

What stood out wasn't just the energy in the room. It was how this group of forward-thinking leaders truly showed up and opened up within such a short time frame.

At every dinner we host, we curate the guest list with intention - not just who we want to meet, but who each person in the room would genuinely want to sit across from, and why it makes sense for everyone at the table to be there.

In one evening, we got to know each other, exchanged notes, and explored upcoming collaborations.

The closers shared what actually moves large deals forward. The conference veterans shared which ones are worth attending - and the secrets to maximizing ROI. The event hosts aligned on future collaborations.

Since community and GTM are, at their core, collaborative, one of the greatest forms of leverage in this work is the network you build. And the most meaningful connections come from rooms like this - dinners structured around genuine relationships as the foundation for what comes next.

Grateful for everyone who made this room what it was. 🙏

Installed wacli and slacrawl into @openclaw and it's been a step up!

Now my CRM has updates from calls (via @meetgranola), from emails, from slack messages, and from WhatsApp. 

OpenClaw knows everything that's going on with me!

It's been so awesome!
